name: AMC Sensory Friendly Films
schedule: 2nd & 4th Saturdays (family films); select Wednesday evenings (mature films)
sensoryFeatures: free to get up, move, talk or sing
parentTip: Use the official page to find a participating AMC near you and confirm the title and showtime — films rotate each month.

name: Regal My Way Matinee (Sensory Friendly)
schedule: Select matinees at participating Regal locations (see schedule)
sensoryFeatures: free to sing, move, talk or dance
parentTip: Search your local Regal on the My Way Matinee page — participating theaters and dates vary by market.

name: Cinemark Sensory Friendly Showtimes
schedule: First Saturday morning of each month at participating locations
parentTip: Availability varies by theater — check your nearest Cinemark's page or call to confirm the sensory-friendly showtime.

name: Chuck E. Cheese Sensory Sensitive Sundays
schedule: First Sunday of each month, opening two hours before normal hours
sensoryFeatures: calmer game room and dining area
cost: Standard play/food pricing; no reservation needed
parentTip: Check the participating-locations page first — not every Chuck E. Cheese runs Sensory Sensitive Sundays, and start times can vary.

name: Snip-its (Autism Support)
schedule: By appointment at quieter times; stylists trained through the Autism Speaks program
sensoryFeatures: stylists trained in autism support (Autism Speaks program)
parentTip: Call your local Snip-its and ask for a quiet-time appointment — they schedule these specifically for kids with sensory needs.

name: Sharkey's Cuts for Kids (Sensory Support)
schedule: Low-sensory hours twice a week; longer appointments on request
sensoryFeatures: weighted lap pads & sensory toys on request
parentTip: Ask the front desk for a weighted lap pad or sensory toy at check-in, and request one of their low-sensory hours when you book.

name: ODEON Autism Friendly Screenings
schedule: One Sunday morning each month (about 11:30am) at 90+ ODEON cinemas — check your local cinema
sensoryFeatures: free to move around, talk and make noise
developed with the autism charity Dimensions
parentTip: Search your nearest ODEON on the autism-friendly page — the exact Sunday and film vary by cinema each month, so confirm before you travel.

name: Cineworld Autism Friendly Screenings
schedule: First Sunday morning of each month (most locations)
sensoryFeatures: relaxed — free to move and make noise
parentTip: Most Cineworlds run these on the first Sunday morning — check your local cinema listings to confirm the title and exact time.

name: Vue Autism Friendly Screenings
schedule: Last Sunday of every month (about 10:30am), at all Vue sites
parentTip: Vue has run these the longest — they fall on the last Sunday each month; book ahead as the popular family titles sell out.

name: ODEON Autism Friendly Screenings (Ireland)
schedule: One Sunday morning each month — check your local ODEON in Ireland
sensoryFeatures: free to move around, talk and make noise
parentTip: Confirm the Sunday and film on the ODEON Ireland page — dates and titles rotate monthly by cinema.

name: Omniplex Sensory Friendly Screenings
schedule: Every weekend (Saturdays in the Republic of Ireland), around 12pm, at all Omniplex cinemas
sensoryFeatures: free to move around during the film
parentTip: Omniplex runs these weekly — a Saturday option most weekends, so you are rarely far from the next screening.

name: IMC Cinemas Sensory Friendly Screenings
schedule: Monthly sensory-friendly screenings — check your local IMC cinema
sensoryFeatures: lights left on low, sound reduced
relaxed atmosphere — moving and noise welcome
designed for guests with autism or sensory disorders
parentTip: IMC schedules these monthly and the date varies by location — check the cinema page or call ahead to confirm.

name: Event Cinemas Sensory Friendly Screenings
schedule: Sundays at selected locations across Australia (not during school holidays)
sensoryFeatures: freedom to move, talk and dance
low-cost tickets for families living with autism
parentTip: Sessions run Sundays at selected cinemas and pause during school holidays — check the Event Cinemas sensory page for the next date near you.

name: HOYTS Sensory Screenings
schedule: Once a month on a Saturday at participating HOYTS cinemas
sensoryFeatures: relaxed cinema rules — get up and move freely
welcoming to guests with autism or sensory difficulties
parentTip: HOYTS runs one sensory Saturday a month — the date and film vary by cinema, so confirm on the HOYTS sensory page before you go.

name: Village Cinemas Sensory Friendly Films
schedule: Sensory-friendly sessions at Village Cinemas (Victoria) — see session times
sensoryFeatures: free to move, talk and respond
specially designed for autistic children, adults and families
parentTip: Village runs these across its Victorian cinemas — check the session finder for your nearest Melbourne-area location.

name: Cineplex Sensory Friendly Screenings
schedule: Select Saturday mornings (about every 4–6 weeks), lights up and sound down, at theatres across Canada
sensoryFeatures: free to get up, move and make noise
parentTip: Cineplex posts titles up to two weeks ahead — screenings run roughly every 4–6 weeks on Saturday mornings, so check the schedule before you plan.

name: Landmark Cinemas Sensory Friendly Films
schedule: About one Saturday a month at Morning Movies pricing — check your local Landmark
sensoryFeatures: screenings for guests with sensory needs
parentTip: Landmark runs roughly one sensory-friendly Saturday a month — the exact date varies by location, so confirm on the cinema page.

name: Urban Air Adventure Park — Sensory Friendly Play
schedule: Sensory Friendly Play events at participating parks — dates vary by location
sensoryFeatures: no loud music and no flashing lights during Sensory Play events
calmer atmosphere designed for kids with autism & sensory processing differences
trampolines, climbing & attractions at a gentler pace
participating locations only — confirm with your local park
cost: See your local park for Sensory Play pricing
parentTip: Call your nearest Urban Air or check its local events page for the next Sensory Friendly Play date — participation and frequency vary by park, so always confirm before promising the trip.

name: Walmart — Daily Sensory-Friendly Hours
schedule: Every day, 8–10am local time at U.S. Walmart stores
sensoryFeatures: radio and overhead audio turned off
TV walls set to a calm static image
quietest, least-crowded hours of the day
parentTip: The 8–10am window is company-wide and permanent, so you can build it into a weekly routine — checkout scanners still beep, so pack the headphones for the register.

name: Sky Zone — Sensory Hours
schedule: Sensory Hours at participating parks — days and times vary by location
sensoryFeatures: quieter music and reduced overhead noise
Calm Down Zone with sensory toys
visual cues placed around the park
participating locations only — confirm with your local park
cost: See your local park for Sensory Hour pricing
parentTip: Call your nearest Sky Zone or check its local page for the next Sensory Hours session — participation varies by park, so always confirm before promising the trip.

name: Marcus Theatres — Reel Movies for Real Needs
schedule: Select Saturday mornings at 11am at participating Marcus Theatres

recurrence.note: 11am on select Saturdays — check the lineup

sensoryFeatures: lower sound and brighter, gentle lighting
first-run family films (plus some PG-13 dates for older kids & adults)
moving around and making noise is welcome — no shushing
runs at a named list of participating theatres — check yours
parentTip: Check the Reel Movies lineup page for the next Saturday title and confirm your theatre is on the participating list before you go.
